name: Preston Jackson
abstract: James Preston McDonald, better known by his stage name Preston Jackson (January 3, 1902 – November 12, 1983) was an American jazz trombonist.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Lovett
about_quotes.evidence: And Art Hodes was there, and Baby Dodds, and Preston Jackson, myself and Bill Price. ... did you ever work with him again? No, I think that was the only time I ever saw him or played with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Wynn
about_quotes.evidence: he gave me all the fundamentals of things... I started to taking lessons from Preston... he just taught me everything that he could about the home.

about_quotes.speaker: Lee Collins
about_quotes.evidence: So I went to work with Joe... So we got up and Joe and Oliver and Joe say, well, you sure surprised me... So we went seven nights a week over gardens.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Darensbourg
about_quotes.evidence: I've heard them. The ones he made with Jimmie Noone and Preston Jackson and Johnny Lindsay and whatnot.

about_quotes.speaker: Happy Caldwell
about_quotes.evidence: Bernie Young on Mike McKendrick trumpet, Mack Hendricks on guitar, Preston Jackson on trombone.

about_quotes.speaker: Willie Hightower
about_quotes.evidence: musicians around here just like, whatever you say Preston Jackson, I always think about him.
